item.title: Measles outbreak: Dhaka division accounts for 50% of total fatalities
item.description: Of the deaths recorded in Dhaka, 38 were confirmed as measles-related, while the remaining 130 were classified as suspected.

item.title: Why mandatory front-of-package labelling is Bangladesh's next public health frontier
Advance Eid-ul-Azha bus ticket sales begin
item.description:
Advance ticket sales for long-distance buses ahead of Eid-ul-Azha have begun.
According to a decision by the Bangladesh Bus-Truck Owners’ Association, ticket sales started on Friday morning through both online platforms and ticket counters.
Advance tickets are being sold for journeys from May 21 until the days leading up to Eid, allowing passengers to book tickets for different routes in advance.
Speaking about the ticket sales, Shyamoli NR Travels General Manager Jibon Chakraborty said ticket sales for all routes began Friday morning. Tickets are available both online and at counters.
He said the highest demand is for journeys on the night of May 24, throughout May 25, and the morning of May 26. Since the government holiday begins on May 25, pressure on tickets during that period is comparatively higher.
He also noted that by 10am, most advance tickets on several routes had already sold out.
Meanwhile, the Bangladesh Bus-Truck Owners’ Association said no additional fare beyond the rates fixed by the Bangladesh Road Transport Authority (BRTA) will be allowed.
The same fare will apply both online and at ticket counters. In addition, displaying BRTA-approved fare charts at every counter will be made mandatory.

item.title: Murdered in US: Flight carrying Bristy’s body leaves Florida for home
President leaves for UK Saturday for medical check-up
item.description:
President Mohammed Shahabuddin is set to leave Dhaka for the United Kingdom (UK) on Saturday for a medical check-up.
“A commercial flight of Biman Bangladesh Airlines carrying the president, his wife Dr Rebeka Sultana, and a small entourage will depart from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport on Saturday morning,” according to the President’s Press Wing.
The president is scheduled to return home on May 17.
He is expected to undergo necessary medical examinations at a specialised hospital in London as per the physicians’ advice.

item.title: Grief grips Faridpur as Beirut airstrike victim Dipali returns home in coffin
Measles child deaths rise in Sylhet
After measles, rabies vaccine crisis hits Bangladesh

Tagore’s 165th birth anniversary being observed
Brazil's Lula and Trump hail positive talks after rocky relations
Shakira teases new World Cup song
Govt declares 7-day holiday for Eid-ul-Azha
Bangladesh seeks global cooperation on safe migration at UN forum
Trump says he would not pay $1,000 to watch US at World Cup
IMO: Some 1,500 ships trapped in Gulf due to Iran conflict
Instagram drops private messaging feature worldwide
item.description:
Instagram users around the world can no longer send highly private direct messages after parent company Meta disabled end-to-end encryption (E2EE) on the platform from Thursday.
The move marks a major shift by Meta, which had earlier promoted the technology as the future standard for online privacy.
End-to-end encryption is considered one of the safest forms of digital communication because only the sender and receiver can read messages. Critics, however, have long argued that it can also make it harder for authorities to detect crimes such as child abuse and online grooming.
With the feature now removed, Instagram will be able to access the contents of direct messages, including photos, videos and voice notes.
Meta had announced plans in 2019 to expand E2EE across Facebook and Instagram messaging services. While Facebook Messenger fully adopted the system in 2023, Instagram only offered it as an optional feature and had planned to make it standard later.
The company has now abandoned those plans after seven years.
Instead, Instagram will use standard encryption, a system commonly used by services such as Gmail, where providers can access data if necessary.
Child safety organisations welcomed the decision. The NSPCC, a UK-based children’s charity, said the removal of E2EE would help prevent criminals from hiding abusive activities online.
“We are really pleased,” said Rani Govender of the NSPCC, adding that encrypted messaging could allow child abuse and grooming to go undetected.
Privacy campaigners strongly criticised the decision, calling it a setback for online security.
Maya Thomas of privacy group Big Brother Watch said E2EE helps protect children’s personal data and warned that Meta may be responding to government pressure.
Meta did not publicly announce the policy reversal but quietly updated Instagram’s terms and conditions in March, stating that end-to-end encrypted messaging would no longer be supported after May 8, 2026.
The company said the decision was made because only a small number of users had enabled the feature.
Cybersecurity experts, however, believe the move may also reflect Meta’s growing interest in using data for targeted advertising and artificial intelligence development.
Victoria Baines, professor of information technology at Gresham College, said social media companies increasingly see user communications as valuable data for AI training and advertising purposes.
Instagram previously said direct messages were not used to train AI systems.
The decision could also affect the wider social media industry, where encrypted messaging had increasingly become common.
Apps such as WhatsApp, Signal, Facebook Messenger, iMessage and Google Messages currently use E2EE by default.
Meanwhile, Telegram offers the feature as optional, while TikTok recently said it has no plans to introduce end-to-end encryption for direct messages.
Experts say Meta’s latest move could slow the wider expansion of encrypted messaging on social media platforms.

item.title: Home is where the heart is
Getting taxation right
Why do people keep dying on our roads?
Five outcomes from the world’s first summit on ending fossil fuels
Top mugging suspect ‘Pankha Rubel’ arrested in Adabor drive
item.description:
Police have arrested listed top mugger and multiple-case accused Rubel alias “Pankha Rubel,” during a special drive in Dhaka’s Adabor area.
He was nabbed around 8:30am on Friday during a raid in the Shekhertek area under Adabor Police Station.
According to police, Rubel is a wanted accused in two warrant cases filed with Adabor Police Station. He also faces nine cases over charges of mugging, robbery, extortion, and narcotics-related offences.
Police said Rubel is known as the second-in-command of the juvenile gang “Rokto Chosha Jony Group,” which operates in the Shekhertek area.
ADC Md Jewel Rana of DMP’s Tejgaon Division said such drives would continue to maintain law and order in the capital.

Religious affairs ministry urges pilgrims to obtain hajj permits through official channels
item.description:
The Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah has urged pilgrims to obtain Hajj permits through officially approved channels and comply with all regulations to ensure smooth and efficient services during the pilgrimage.
In line with the directive, Bangladesh’s Ministry of Religious Affairs has also requested Bangladeshi pilgrims to secure Hajj permits through official procedures.
The information was disclosed in a statement issued on Friday.
According to the statement, the Saudi ministry said Hajj can only be performed with a valid Hajj visa and stressed the importance of obtaining official permits through authorized procedures after arriving in Saudi Arabia to ensure proper management and discipline during the pilgrimage.
The ministry noted that compliance with permit requirements would allow pilgrims to access all services at the holy sites while enabling authorities to manage crowds, regulate movement, and strengthen overall safety through an integrated system.
Saudi authorities also warned pilgrims against obtaining Hajj permits or visas through unauthorized channels or fraudulent offers.
The ministry said such unauthorized practices could hinder pilgrims from properly performing religious rituals and may expose them to legal penalties.
Emphasizing the importance of securing permits through institutional channels, the Saudi Ministry of Hajj and Umrah said the measures are intended to ensure a smooth Hajj experience and guarantee access to services in a safe, secure, and well-organized environment.

item.title: A journey to Rarikhal: Seeking the soul of JC Bose
Experts sound alarm over failing education system
Poverty, poor learning trap students in crisis
Nahid warns of regional impact after West Bengal election results

Govt approves draft offshore PSC to boost oil, gas exploration
Budget session set to begin June 7
Dhaka air again world’s most polluted Friday morning
item.description:
The densely populated capital of Bangladesh has once again topped the list of cities with the worst air quality in the world.
The Air Quality Index (AQI) in Dhaka was recorded at 183 at 9:30am on Friday.
India’s Delhi, Pakistan’s Lahore and Indonesia’s Jakarta ranked second, third and fourth respectively, with AQI scores of 153, 153 and 148.
An AQI reading between 101 and 200 is considered ‘unhealthy’, particularly for sensitive groups. A reading between 201 and 300 is considered ‘very unhealthy’, while 301 to 400 is classified as ‘hazardous’, posing serious health risks.
The AQI is used by government agencies to report daily air quality and inform the public about pollution levels and possible health impacts.
In Bangladesh, the index is calculated based on five major pollutants — particulate matter (PM10 and PM2.5), nitrogen dioxide (NO₂), carbon monoxide (CO), sulphur dioxide (SO₂) and ozone.
Dhaka has long struggled with severe air pollution, with conditions typically worsening in winter and improving during the monsoon season.
Air pollution remains one of the leading global health risks. Exposure to polluted air increases the risk of heart disease, chronic respiratory illness, lung infections and cancer, according to multiple studies.
The World Health Organization (WHO) estimates that air pollution causes around seven million premature deaths worldwide each year, mainly due to stroke, heart disease, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, lung cancer and acute respiratory infections.

Love finds its way through chance in ‘Tumi Ami Shudhu’
PM orders accurate list of rain-hit haor farmers for assistance
item.description:
Prime Minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday directed the authorities concerned to prepare a transparent and accurate list of farmers affected by recent heavy rainfall in the haor region to ensure proper financial support reaches the genuine victims.
The directive came at a meeting held at the prime minister’s Office in the Cabinet Division at the Bangladesh Secretariat in the afternoon.
The meeting reviewed the government’s planned support program for farmers in the haor areas who suffered crop losses due to recent heavy rains, said a release issued by the prime minister’s Press Wing.
Financial assistance will be provided to the farmers for the next three months based on this list, it said.
The meeting also discussed slightly advancing the paddy planting and harvesting seasons to help farmers avoid losses caused by heavy rainfall.
On April 29, while speaking in parliament, the prime minister said the government would try to provide three months of assistance to farmers affected by heavy rainfall in the Haor region.
Disaster Management and Relief Minister Asadul Habib Dulu and Agriculture, Food, Fisheries and Livestock Minister Aminur Rashid Yasin attended the meeting.
MPs from the Haor region, including Deputy Speaker of Parliament Barrister Kayser Kamal, State Minister for Textiles and Jute Shariful Alam, Dr Osman Faruk, Fazlur Rahman, Lutfozzaman Babar, Kamruzzaman Kamrul, Nurul Islam, Kalim Uddin Milon and Kaisar M Ahmed were, among others, present at the meeting.

item.title: IPDC Finance launches premium financial segment ‘IPDC Infinite’
item.description:
Country’s first private sector financial institution, IPDC Finance PLC, launched “IPDC Infinite” in Dhaka on May 7, 2026, introducing an exclusive premium financial segment designed to deliver a more personalized and elevated experience for high-net-worth customers.
Built around customers’ financial aspirations, lifestyle preferences and evolving expectations, IPDC Infinite has been structured into two tiers — Elite and Infinite. Through the proposition, customers will have access to tailored financial solutions as well as a premium lifestyle ecosystem.
The segment offers a wide range of exclusive privileges, including dedicated relationship managers, doorstep banking services, priority customer support, airport lounge access, premium travel and dining offers, healthcare benefits and other curated services aimed at enhancing convenience and exclusivity.
Speaking on the occasion, Rizwan Dawood Shams, managing director of IPDC Finance PLC, said: “The Launching of IPDC Infinite marks an important step forward in strengthening deeper and more meaningful relationships with our customers. Today’s premium customers seek more than just financial services — they value recognition, personalized experiences, and distinguished service standards. Through IPDC Infinite, we aim to redefine and deliver on those expectations.”
The launch reflects IPDC Finance’s continued focus on experience-driven relationship management, where long-term customer relationships, exclusivity and personalized care remain central to its service philosophy.
To further enrich customer experience, IPDC Finance has partnered with leading hospitality, travel, dining, healthcare and lifestyle brands across the country to offer a curated range of premium privileges and benefits.
Through the launch of “IPDC Infinite,” IPDC Finance said it aims to set a new benchmark in Bangladesh’s premium financial services landscape by combining financial excellence with elevated lifestyle experiences.

item.title: Religious affairs ministry urges compliance with hajj regulations in Saudi Arabia
item.description:
Saudi Arabia has introduced stringent regulations for the upcoming Hajj season, including heavy penalties for violations, as part of efforts to manage overcrowding and enhance pilgrim safety. The Saudi authorities have also reiterated warnings against performing Hajj without official authorization.
In a statement issued on Thursday, the Ministry of Religious Affairs of Bangladesh confirmed the matter, according to a press release signed by its Public Relations Officer Md Abu Bakar Siddique. The ministry has urged Bangladeshi citizens to strictly comply with the new Saudi regulations.
According to the statement, the Saudi government has imposed strict penalties for attempts to perform Hajj without valid permits. Foreign residents found violating the rules may face deportation along with a re-entry ban of up to 10 years. Individuals attempting to perform Hajj without authorization could be fined up to 20,000 Saudi riyals.
In addition, those found facilitating unauthorized pilgrims by providing transport, accommodation, or any form of assistance will also face legal consequences. Depending on the level of involvement, fines may reach up to 100,000 Saudi riyals. Authorities have further stated that vehicles used in such activities may be confiscated under applicable laws.
Under the revised regulations, only officially authorized pilgrims will be permitted to enter Makkah and other holy sites. The restrictions will apply to all pilgrims and residents within Saudi Arabia alike.
The enforcement period will run from the first day of Zilqad until the 14th of Zilhajj. During this time, individuals holding visit visas or lacking valid Hajj permits will not be allowed entry into or stay within Makkah.

item.title: ISU honors Prof Hakikur Rahman for outstanding research contributions
item.description:
International Standard University (ISU) organized a ceremony on Thursday to recognize distinguished contributions to research and international academic excellence, where Professor Dr Md Hakikur Rahman, Chairman of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE), was honored.
The program was held at the university’s Media Lab and organized by the Department of CSE. ISU Vice-Chancellor Professor Dr Abdul Awal Khan presided over the event, while Professor Dr Harikumar Pallathadka, Chancellor of Manipur International University, India, attended as the chief guest. He formally presented the PhD Honorary Recognition Crest to Professor Dr Md Hakikur Rahman.
In his remarks, Vice-Chancellor Professor Dr Abdul Awal Khan reaffirmed ISU’s strong commitment to research, innovation, and knowledge-based education. He emphasized that research-led education plays a vital role in national development and in strengthening global competitiveness.
The chief guest, Professor Dr Harikumar Pallathadka, highlighted that research excellence reflects the true strength and global reputation of a university. He praised Professor Dr Md Hakikur Rahman’s significant contributions to international research and noted that such recognition would inspire young scholars to pursue research with greater dedication.
In his response, Professor Dr Md Hakikur Rahman expressed gratitude for the honor, stating that research is not only a personal achievement but also a continuous responsibility to apply knowledge for the betterment of society and humanity. He added that the recognition would further motivate him to advance his research work with renewed commitment.
The event was attended by Professor Dr Monjur Morshed Mahmud, Dean of the Faculty of Business Studies; Professor Md Abul Kashem, Dean of the Faculty of Engineering and Technology; Professor Dr Mohammad Ekramul Haque, Director of IQAC; Professor Mohammad Ali of the Department of Business Administration; Registrar Md Faizullah Koushik; along with department heads, faculty members, students, and other university officials.

item.title: Two months after fare charts installation, DU's rickshaw regulation project remains unimplemented
item.description:
Two months after fare charts were installed across the Dhaka University campus as part of a plan to introduce uniformly dressed, non-smoking rickshaw pullers operating under fixed rates, the initiative remains unimplemented.
The project, an initiative of the university administration implemented in coordination with the Dhaka University Central Students’ Union (DUCSU) and supported by Green Future Foundation, was designed to bring order to campus transport through registered drivers, standard uniforms, and regulated fares. However, despite preparatory steps, it has yet to be formally launched.
DUCSU officials say they have completed all preparations on their end, including selecting rickshaw pullers, preparing fare charts, and distributing vests. According to them, the delay now lies with the administration.
The university administration, however, says that around 50 of the 100 non-smoking rickshaw pullers on their approved list have since started operating unauthorised battery-powered vehicles. They say they hope to launch the project either before or after the upcoming Eid.
DUCSU's Transport Secretary Asif Abdullah told the Dhaka Tribune, "Everything is ready on our end, we're all set to go — we just need staff posted at the campus entry and exit points, which the university administration hasn't been able to provide yet." DU Estate Manager Fatema Binte Mostofa said the implementation process has taken longer than expected.
“Whenever we take up such initiatives, it takes three to four months just to organise everything. In the meantime, unauthorised battery-powered rickshaws became quite popular,” she said.
"Of the 100 non-smoking rickshaw pullers we had listed, about 50 of them have already switched to those unauthorised battery-powered vehicles. So now we have to put together a fresh list of young, non-smoking rickshaw pullers."
When asked for a more concrete timeline, the Estate Manager said it could happen either before or after the upcoming Eid ul-Adha. Finding a DU student who hasn't had a frustrating experience haggling over rickshaw fares on campus is no easy task. Even for short distances, students end up paying well over the odds. Things have now reached a point where fares are essentially unregulated — with no oversight or fixed rate system in place, drivers are charging whatever they feel like, and students, left with no real alternative, have little choice but to pay up.
Currently, fares for short trips that previously cost around Tk 20 are often no less than Tk 30, with drivers setting prices arbitrarily in the absence of a functioning regulatory system.
Earlier on December 15, 2024, DU Proctor Saifuddin Ahmed announced that only registered rickshaws would be allowed to operate inside the campus in an effort to regulate movement and manage external access.
Before that, on May 18, 2023, following complaints from students and teachers — and under the initiative of Bangladesh Chhatra League, now a banned organisation — the university administration had introduced a fixed fare structure for the campus. Rates were set based on distance, ranging from a minimum of 15 taka to a maximum of 40 taka.

item.title: Prof Almujaddade Alfasane appointed DU Pro VC
item.description:
Professor Dr Mohammed Almujaddade Alfasane of the Botany Department has been appointed the new pro-vice-chancellor (Administration) of Dhaka University.
A gazette notification issued by the Ministry of Education on Thursday announced the appointment, subject to four conditions.
He replaces Professor Sayema Haque Bidisha, who was appointed to the post during the interim government.
According to the notification, issued with the approval of the president and chancellor under Article 13(1) of the Dhaka University Order, 1973, Alfasane’s tenure will be four years from the date of joining.
He will receive a salary equivalent to his current position, along with other benefits in line with existing rules, and must reside on campus. He will exercise powers and perform responsibilities assigned under university statutes, laws and directives from the vice-chancellor. The president and chancellor may revoke the appointment at any time if necessary.
A former secretary of the Dhaka University Club, Alfasane served as convener of the Faculty of Biological Sciences under Shada Dal, the BNP-Jamaat-backed teachers’ panel at the university.
He is a former secretary general of the Nationalist Research Foundation and currently serves as joint secretary general of the University Teachers’ Association of Bangladesh (UTAB). He is also an adviser to the Ziaur Rahman Foundation.
Alfasane completed his undergraduate and postgraduate studies in the Botany Department at Dhaka University before joining the faculty. He later earned his PhD from the same university.

Nur: Doctors studied with public funds must uphold their duty to the people
item.description:
Nurul Haque Nur, state minister for Expatriates’ Welfare and Overseas Employment and president of Patuakhali Medical College Hospital, has said that students studying at government medical colleges have a moral obligation to serve the people, as their education is funded by taxpayers’ money. He made the remarks while addressing at a view- exchange meeting with doctors, students, and staff members of the hospital on Thursday noon at Patuakhali Medical College Hospital.
Nur noted that studying government medical costs significantly less than at private medical colleges, where expenses range from Tk 4 million to Tk 7 million.
“The state bears this expense through the VAT and taxes paid by farmers, workers, and ordinary citizens. After becoming doctors with public funds, you should fulfill your professional responsibilities and serve the people,” he said.
He also urged future doctors to uphold humanity, ethics, and moral responsibility in their profession.
“If you work with humanity, ethics, and a sense of moral responsibility, you will earn people’s blessings and the appreciation of the Creator,” he added.
After visiting the hospital, Nur said the government is prioritizing the development of the education, healthcare, and employment sectors, adding that progress in these areas would significantly advance the country.
He said initiatives have already been taken to address the shortage of doctors and healthcare personnel in hospitals across Bangladesh. The government is also planning to introduce health cards for citizens alongside various digital healthcare services, including e-health facilities.
According to the minister, staffing shortages at district and upazila-level hospitals will soon be resolved through new recruitment drives.
Referring to the 250-bed Patuakhali Medical College Hospital, he said the hospital is regularly treating nearly three times its capacity, which is affecting the quality of healthcare services provided to patients. He assured that a new 500-bed hospital would be constructed alongside Patuakhali Medical College to expand healthcare services in the region. Among those present during the visit were Abu Yousuf, Deputy Secretary of Local Government Jewel Rana, Civil Surgeon Dr Khaledur Rahman Mia, Superintendent of Patuakhali Medical College Hospital Dr Dilruba Yasmin Liza, and Vice Principal of Patuakhali Medical College Dr Atiqur Rahman, along with heads of various government departments.

item.title: Deputy speaker: Parliamentary caucus to work with Unicef on child rights
item.description:
Deputy Speaker Barrister Kayser Kamal on Thursday said the Parliamentary Caucus on Child Rights and Unicef will work jointly to ensure the protection of child rights in Bangladesh.
“A caucus will be formed comprising the chairpersons of relevant parliamentary standing committees and members of parliament from both the treasury and opposition benches to safeguard children's interests and formulate child-friendly policies,” he said.
He made these remarks during a courtesy meeting with a Unicef delegation led by its Social Policy Manager Hasina Begum at his office at the National Parliament.
The deputy speaker expressed optimism that this caucus would play a pivotal role in parliamentary discussions regarding child rights and in securing increased budget allocations for the sector.
Barrister Kayser Kamal highlighted that Prime Minister Tarique Rahman is highly sensitive to child rights issues.
“The prime minister has already issued necessary directives to allow children the opportunity to observe parliamentary sessions from the galleries,” he noted.
The Unicef delegation informed the deputy speaker that they have successfully advocated for the inclusion of 10 priority rights for children—including health, education, and protection—in the election manifestos of various political parties.
They also lauded the government’s timely measures in addressing the ongoing measles situation.
During the meeting, both sides discussed a wide range of issues, including child-friendly policy formulation, increased social protection, budgetary enhancements, and bilateral cooperation.

item.title: Ex-DGFI official Manzil Haider remanded in uprising killing case
item.description:
A Dhaka court on Thursday placed former Directorate General of Forces Intelligence (DGFI) official Major (retd) Manzil Haider Chowdhury on a three-day remand in a case filed over the killing of garment worker Rubel in the capital's Adabor Ring Road area during the July Mass Uprising.
Dhaka Metropolitan Magistrate Mohammad Ehsanul Islam passed the order as police produced Manzil before the court and pleaded to place him on a seven-day remand.
Police arrested Manzil Haider Chowdhury from Mohakhali DOHS area on April. He was placed a four-day remand in the case on May 3.
According to the case documents, a couple of hundred student-people including Rubel brought out a rally in Adabor Ring Road area in the capital on August 5, 2025. Around 11:30am, the rally came under attack from police and cadres of Awami League and its front organizations. Rubel got shot and died on the spot.
Rubel's father Rafikul Islam filed the case on August 22 with the Adabor Police Station.

item.title: BB issues consolidated policy on forex management in transport sector
item.description:
Bangladesh Bank has issued new consolidated policy on foreign exchange management in the case of sending money outside the country against international passenger and cargo transport services.
A circular issued on Thursday has given uniform instructions for foreign exchange transactions related to international passenger transport, foreign airlines, shipping companies and cargo services related to ticket and charge collection.
The central bank said that through this initiative, a consolidated regulatory framework has been introduced in foreign exchange management in the transport sector.
As a result, procedural complexity will be reduced, compliance with regulations will be easier and ambiguities due to separate instructions will be removed.
The circular said that foreign exchange transactions and outward remittance activities will be conducted under the Foreign Exchange Control Act, 1947 and related circulars.
However, to simplify the activities, all the previous instructions related to the transport sector have been brought together and included in a consolidated document.
At the same time, amendments have also been made to some provisions as necessary.
Bangladesh Bank said that this circular has been issued under Section 20(3) of the Foreign Exchange Control Act.
It will remain in effect for one year from the date of issuance. New instructions issued during this period will also be considered in conjunction with this framework.
The new instructions include the issuance of tickets for international passenger transport and cargo services, collection of freight charges, foreign currency transactions of foreign airlines and shipping companies, and transactions of state-owned enterprises—Bangladesh Shipping Corporation and Biman Bangladesh Airlines.
In addition, separate instructions have been given for private shipping companies, airlines, courier services and freight forwarders.
Provisions for operating and managing foreign currency accounts of shipping companies, airlines and freight forwarding agencies have also been added to it.
Foreign currency accounts operated in the name of Bangladeshi transport companies conducting international activities have also been brought under the new policy.
The circular has also given updated instructions for tour operators.
According to Bangladesh Bank, the main objective of this initiative is to increase transparency in the rapidly expanding services sector and ensure consistency with the regulatory framework.
A central bank official said that the new integrated framework will make the outward remittance process in the transport and logistics sector easier, more transparent and more efficient.
At the same time, it will be possible to ensure compliance with foreign exchange regulations.

Stop raising taxes
The world must not forget the Rohingya
Legal notice seeks ban on social media use for under-16s
item.description:
A legal notice has been served seeking a ban on the use of social media by children and adolescents under the age of 16, along with effective measures to prevent misuse and strengthen regulatory control.
The notice was sent by Barrister Sanaullah Noori on behalf of the Legal Voice Foundation to the secretaries of the Posts and Telecommunications Division and the Information and Communication Technology Division, as well as the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission (BTRC) on Thursday.
According to the notice, the use of social media platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, TikTok, Snapchat and YouTube is taking an “alarmingly negative direction,” with children and adolescents increasingly misusing these platforms.
It said social media-driven violence and addiction have become widespread, causing children to lose interest in sports and suffer physical and mental health problems.
The notice further said the spread of misinformation, propaganda and easy access to pornographic content are having a serious negative impact on the minds and intellect of children and adolescents.
As a result, many are becoming inattentive to their studies and reluctant to attend school and college, leading to a decline in moral values, it added.
The notice also claimed that children and adolescents are becoming involved in various criminal activities, including group-based offences, due to the misuse of digital media.
It alleged that addiction to pornography and online games is pushing many away from moral education and towards criminal behaviour, often beyond the control of guardians.
The notice added that many parents and guardians lack adequate knowledge about digital platforms and are therefore unable to effectively monitor or control misuse.
It called on the authorities concerned to establish effective control over social media and take practical steps to prevent misuse within 15 days of receiving the notice.

item.title: Inflation rises over 9% again in April
Bank Asia holds mental health awareness program for employees
Mohanlal returns as past resurfaces in Drishyam 3
Power companies push for electricity price; hearings set for May 20-21
CNN founder Ted Turner dies at 87
Landmark ‘Deposit Protection Act, 2026’ enacted to ensure financial stability
Dhaka sees evening downpour after hot day
PM calls for people-oriented administration, faster hassle-free public services
Workshop held at RU Philosophy Department
Two Juba Dal leaders expelled over RDA tender box snatching
RMG workers block Dhaka–Sylhet highway demanding unpaid wages
Williamson named in New Zealand squad for Ireland, England Tests
item.description:
Kane Williamson will continue his pursuit of 10,000 Test runs after being named Thursday in New Zealand's squad to play Ireland and England in May and June.
The squad features a shock omission from the England leg of the tour, Will Young left out in favour of Henry Nicholls.
The 19-man squad will play a one-off Test against Ireland starting May 27 in Belfast before a three-Test series against England starting June 4.
Williamson has 9,461 runs at an average of 54.7 in 108 Test matches, and said in December he was considering retirement.
"As you get to the latter stages (of your career) those thoughts certainly enter your mind," said Williamson, who added his future commitment to the Black Caps would be "series by series".
As it turns out the 35-year-old has committed to New Zealand's next four Tests, while New Zealand will also play India at home and Australia away later in the year.
Young's omission for the England Tests comes as a shock given he'd been the long-standing preferred back-up to New Zealand's batting group.
With limited opportunities, Young had four 50-plus scores in his last nine Test innings, including 74 opening the batting against a fiery spell from fast bowler Blessing Muzarabani on a lively pitch against Zimbabwe.
Nicholls, who had earned a recall after 18 months in the international wilderness, came in the next day on a then-placid pitch and scored 150 not out, but having barely faced Zimbabwe's best bowler.
Young is part of the squad for the Ireland Test but will head home at its conclusion.
Black Caps coach Rob Walter named two potential debutants in seamer Kristian Clarke and spin-bowling all-rounder Dean Foxcroft.
"To be able to test ourselves against some of the very best Test teams in the world is an exciting challenge and with the added carrot of the fourth edition of the World Test Championship," Walter said.
"I know it's something the players and staff alike have been looking forward to for some time and we're determined to meet the opportunity head on."
New Zealand are bolstered by the return of Kyle Jamieson and Will O'Rourke, while Jacob Duffy will sit out the tour as he awaits the birth of his child.
All-rounder Michael Bracewell said he will no longer play Test cricket, instead focusing on limited-overs formats.
New Zealand: Tom Latham (capt), Tom Blundell, Kristian Clarke (IRE only), Devon Conway, Zak Foulkes, Dean Foxcroft, Matt Henry, Kyle Jamieson, Daryl Mitchell, Henry Nicholls, Will O'Rourke, Glenn Phillips, Michael Rae (IRE only), Rachin Ravindra, Ben Sears, Nathan Smith, Blair Tickner, Kane Williamson, Will Young (IRE only).

item.title: PSG switch focus to Arsenal after 'hardest match of season'
item.description:
Paris Saint‑Germain winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia said his side had already turned their attention to Champions League final opponents Arsenal after seeing off Bayern Munich on Wednesday.
PSG set up a showdown with the Premier League leaders as a 1-1 draw in the second leg at Bayern sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory.
The Georgian said sinking the German giants was the most difficult task of the season so far.
"Very happy we're in the final. We know it will be difficult against Arsenal. Bayern are one of the best teams right now – it was very difficult, the hardest one of the season," Kvaratskhelia told Canal Plus.
"We showed we can play against these teams."
Scorer of two goals in the first leg, Ousmane Dembele broke through after just three minutes in Munich, turning in a pinpoint Kvaratskhelia cutback.
After the early strike, PSG remained dangerous but had a two-goal lead in the tie and were also content to sit back and control the match.
Harry Kane's stoppage-time goal, his 55th of the season, came too late for the hosts, with PSG advancing to the showpiece in Budapest on May 30.
PSG coach Luis Enrique praised his side's spirit against Bayern, who desperately tried to force their way back into the tie but could not break through.
"The character we showed against a team like Bayern is so positive. We're so happy to reach a second Champions League final in a row," Luis Enrique told reporters.
"It was very intense. Very difficult. They play football at the highest level. Both teams are similar, we love to press high. We are very happy."

item.title: Neymar and Robinho Jr publicly reconcile after altercation
item.description:
Neymar has publicly apologized to Santos team-mate Robinho Junior for slapping him during a training session, as the pair appeared to put the dispute behind them during a game on Tuesday.
Santos said they had opened an investigation earlier in the week following the altercation between Neymar and the son of Robinho, the former Real Madrid and Manchester City striker.
"If you want an apology in front of the press, here it is. I had already apologized to him and to his family. I crossed the line," Neymar told reporters after Santos' 1–1 draw against Recoleta in Paraguay.
According to Brazilian media, the spat occurred because Neymar, the national team's all-time leading scorer, did not appreciate being dribbled past by his 18-year-old team-mate during a training session on Sunday.
Robinho Jr confirmed after the Copa Sudamericana match on Tuesday that Neymar had slapped him, but said the incident was over, stating: "Everything is resolved."
"It's a situation that upset me because he's been my idol since childhood... I talked about it with my parents and I accept his apology," he added.
During the match, Neymar scored Santos' goal and hugged Robinho Jr during the celebration.
"He's a boy I like very much, for whom I have a special affection. It happens in football; you argue with your friend, your brother," said Neymar.
Despite his recurring physical problems, Neymar, 34, still hopes to be called up by coach Carlo Ancelotti for this year's World Cup, which runs from June 11 to July 19. He has not played for Brazil since October 2023.
Ancelotti is due to name his final squad on May 18.
A former teammate of Neymar at Santos, Robinho, 42, is currently imprisoned in Brazil after being convicted in Italy and sentenced to prison for gang rape.

item.title: Manchester City win Women's Super League title after 10 years
item.description:
Manchester City were crowned Women's Super League champions for the first time in 10 years without playing after Arsenal were held to a 1-1 draw at Brighton on Wednesday.
Third-placed Arsenal were eager to bounce back from Saturday's Champions League exit to Lyon and keep the title race alive in the first of three matches in hand on City.
Arsenal boss Renee Slegers knew only victory at Brighton would be enough but the visitors were unable to find a winner after Frida Maanum's 62nd-minute strike cancelled out Fuka Tsunoda's first-half opener.
The 1-1 draw signalled an end to Arsenal's title challenge and Chelsea's run of six consecutive league championships.
It means City celebrate silverware in their first season under boss Andree Jeglertz with their first WSL title since 2016.
"Helping guide this team to the WSL title is something I will never forget," said Jeglertz.
"The girls have met every challenge in front of them head on and have been an absolute joy to coach this season.
"In the good moments and the bad, they've always stuck together and found a way to win –- something that all great champions do."
